---
title: "Zarr (planned)"
---

::: {.callout-note}
**Planned guide -- not yet written.** A beginner's guide to Zarr (chunked,
compressed, cloud-native N-dimensional arrays) may be adapted from the
zarr-python beginner's guide drafted elsewhere.
:::

## Intended scope

- What Zarr is and the problem it solves (cloud-native array storage).
- Stores, groups, arrays, chunks, and compression.
- Metadata: `zarr.json`, `dimension_names`, attributes.
- Zarr v2 vs. v3 in brief.
- How Zarr relates to NetCDF/HDF5 and to Xarray.
- Where coordinate/CRS semantics come from (GeoZarr, conventions).
